title: Domain Evidence, Regulation, Standards, and Licensing Boundaries · Stakeholder Case: Compliance Nonclaim
summary: The product depends on health, finance, education, civic, creative, or safety assumptions inferred from secondary summaries and incompatible licenses. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone compliance nonclaim increment in domain authority and compliance boundary dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.
Trace source, dependency, data, content, model, font, media, and generated-artifact licenses into distribution obligations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
theory case — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Compliance Nonclaim contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions. For Predict Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim, keep Compliance Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Compliance Nonclaim condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct — Controls public-good reasoning, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, security, competence, review, and accountability.

hints: For Predict Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Compliance Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Predict Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.

For Predict Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Meeting a technical checklist permits a compliance claim.

options.text: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Meeting a technical checklist permits a compliance claim. That claim does not survive the predict compliance nonclaim evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Bound Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
theory case — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Compliance Nonclaim application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions. For Bound Compliance Nonclaim · Theory Compliance Nonclaim, keep Compliance Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The worked example variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate implemented controls and collected evidence from formal compliance, certification, approval, or legal conclusions. A defensible solution applies Compliance Nonclaim, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to domain authority and compliance boundary dossier.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-3
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Meeting a technical checklist permits a compliance claim. This fails the Compliance Nonclaim contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct (ACM Code 2018 current) — Controls public-good reasoning, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, security, competence, review, and accountability.

content.label: Mastery evidence

steps.title: Build Compliance Nonclaim evidence · Theory Compliance Nonclaim
